sourceMovie.title: Blue Moon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Blue Moon for the same reason it broke through in 2025: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Richard Linklater keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The history and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Annie Hall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blue Moon clicked for you, Annie Hall (1977) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Blue Moon?
faq.answer: Try Annie Hall, Coyote Ugly, The Last Emperor—each shares DNA with Blue Moon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Blue Moon?
faq.answer: Blue Moon (2025) is best described as History, Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Blue Moon worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
